title: Mythos finds 271 Firefox bugs as solar crosses 30% - TCR 04/22/26
description:
Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview identified 271 security vulnerabilities in Firefox 150's unreleased code - a twelvefold jump over the previous model in one month - while Meta began capturing employee keystrokes to train autonomous agents and a federal judge restored 57 GW of blocked clean energy.
This episode covers the Mythos security audit, the containment breach through contractor credentials, the White House reversal on Anthropic, Meta's behavioral data capture program, the 57 GW wind and solar injunction, NERC's Level 3 grid alert, and the first triple-junction perovskite-on-silicon solar cell to exceed 30% efficiency.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-22-2026/

0:00 What shipped
0:23 Mythos finds 271 vulnerabilities in Firefox 150
3:46 Containment breach through contractor credentials
10:25 NERC Level 3 alert on data center grid instability
13:48 What Shipped

Mozilla disclosed that Mythos found 271 vulnerabilities in Firefox 150 before it shipped - twelve times the 22 bugs its predecessor found in Firefox 148 one month earlier - and Firefox CTO Bobby Holley described the moment as the first time vulnerability discovery speed has overtaken accumulation speed in production software. Hours later, Anthropic confirmed unauthorized users accessed Mythos through a contractor's credentials, breaching the Project Glasswing containment architecture on its first day. The administration completed a six-week reversal from "supply chain risk" to "can be of great use," while Sam Altman compared Mythos to "building a bomb while selling a bomb shelter." Meta deployed passive keystroke, mouse, and screenshot capture on U.S. employee computers to train agents that CTO Andrew Bosworth says will "primarily do the work," with 10% layoffs beginning May 20. A federal judge struck down five administrative actions blocking 57 GW of wind and solar development, finding them "arbitrary and capricious." NERC is preparing a Level 3 essential actions alert after data center load losses exceeding 1,000 MW disrupted the bulk power system. And a triple-junction perovskite-on-silicon solar cell published in *Nature* crossed 30% efficiency for the first time, joining JinkoSolar's 32.76% industrial result and an autonomous perovskite discovery system from April 15 in a feedback loop that is compressing the solar efficiency frontier.
